text:
St Mary's Hospital

Institute of Neurology

Clinic Date: 14 October 2025

Dr Patel
St Mary's Hospital
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY

Dear Dr Patel,

Liam Thompson, DOB: 12-05-1986, Hospital No: H938417 NHS No. 9052174631
Flat 4, 23 Forge Street, London, W10 6AB

Medication:

Levetiracetam 1 g twice daily. No adverse effects reported. Patient carries a midazolam buccal rescue pack but has not needed to use it.

History since last review:

He reports that his current pattern remains stable with a complex partial seizure once every two months. Episodes are described as a brief warning of "time slowing" followed by behavioural arrest and impaired awareness for 1–2 minutes, with post-event fatigue for up to an hour. No secondary generalisation has been witnessed. His partner has observed occasional right-hand fumbling during events.

Occupational context:

He works in metal fabrication and welding with intermittent arc-light exposure. He states he consistently uses a protective visor and adheres to workplace safety procedures. He has not had any events while actively welding; two of the last three episodes occurred at home in the evening, one at work during a tea break away from active machinery. He denies missed doses around these times. He notes possible trigger of poor sleep prior to the workday with early shifts.

Plan:

- Continue levetiracetam 1 g twice daily given current stability and tolerability.
- Reinforce adherence and sleep hygiene; he will keep a diary noting sleep, shifts, and any events to help identify modifiable factors.
- He will maintain standard workplace precautions already in place, including supervised breaks and avoidance of lone working during any prodromal symptoms as agreed with his line manager (patient-reported).
- No driving; he understands the current regulations and will inform us of any change in frequency or event characteristics.

Follow-up in six months, or sooner if the pattern changes.

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: current pattern remains stable with a complex partial seizure once every two months
complex partial seizure once every two months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "current pattern remains stable with a complex partial seizure once every two months." This provides a clear numeric frequency. No other seizure type/frequency is mentioned that would be more frequent. Therefore, normalize "once every two months" to the allowed format as "1 per 2 month."

reference: complex partial seizure once every two months
